title: Double out_rc check distinguishes transport failure from server-side failure
description: auth.rs, nvm.rs: every wolfHSM call returns two error codes: the transport RC (function return value) and an out_rc written by the server. Both are checked separately with distinct func strings, e.g. 'wh_Client_AuthLogin' vs 'wh_Client_AuthLogin(server)'. A transport error means the request never reached the server; a server-side error means it was received and rejected. Conflating them produces misleading diagnostics. A future contributor might suggest checking only the non-zero one. That loses failure-location information. The (server) suffix convention is cheap and unambiguous.
